methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.0B - $1.5B.

data.narrative:
Dream Security, a Series B defense and security company founded by Shalev Hulio and Sebastian Kurz, is valued at $1.5B post-money following $100M+ in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of ten firms: two public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and eight private companies in the same sector and stage, including Vannevar Labs, Harmattan AI, Castelion, Defense Unicorns, and Heven AeroTech.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $1.5B last post-money valuation, implying $2.9B. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range of $1.0B to $1.5B. This peer set includes valuations for Vannevar Labs at $1.5B, Harmattan AI at $1.4B, and three peers at $1.0B each.

The triangulated analysis yields a low estimate of $850M, a median of $1.9B, and a high of $3.3B. The $1.1B spread between peer-median and stage-progression methods reflects tension between relative valuation anchoring and forward-stage expectations. Key uncertainties include company-specific performance relative to peer trajectory and the appropriate step-up multiple for this sector at the Series B-to-later-stage transition.
